properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued May 10 at 9:40PM CDT until May 10 at 11:45PM CDT by NWS Austin/San Antonio TX
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall continues.

* WHERE...A portion of south central Texas, including the following
counties, Burnet and Llano.

* WHEN...Until 1145 PM CDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 940 PM CDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated earlier heavy rainfall due to thunderstorms. This
will cause urban and small stream flooding. Between 1 and 2.5
inches of rain has fallen with the highest rainfall amounts
near Tow and Inks Lake State Park. The LCRA gauge at 5 west
of Tow reported 2.20 inches of rainfall since 8:10 PM.
- Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5 inches are expected
over the area. This additional rain will result in minor
flooding.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Burnet, Marble Falls, Kingsland, Llano, Buchanan Dam,
Gainesville, Granite Shoals, Horseshoe Bay, Buchanan Lake
Village, Highland Haven, Stolz, Bluffton, Lone Grove, Tow,
Inks Lake State Park, Baby Head, Longhorn Cavern State Park,
Valley Spring, Field Creek and Sunrise Beach Village.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

NWSheadline: FLOOD ADVISORY REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 1145 PM CDT THIS EVENING

* WHEN...Until early Friday morning.

Shoals gage reading is over 17 feet.  Private levees may be

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

feet.

ending at 9:45 PM EDT Sunday /8:45 PM CDT Sunday/ was 18.9
feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise to a crest of 18.9
feet early tomorrow afternoon. It will then fall below flood
stage Thursday afternoon.
- Flood stage is 16.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Heat Advisory issued May 10 at 7:32PM PDT until May 12 at 8:00PM PDT by NWS Hanford CA
properties.description:
* WHAT...High temperatures up to 102 with widespread Moderate
HeatRisk expected. Areas of Major HeatRisk will impact portions of
the foothills where the thermal belt exists overnight and in urban
areas including Fresno.

* WHERE...San Joaquin Valley, Coastal Ranges, as well as Lake
Isabella and the Kern River Valley.

* WHEN...From 11 AM Monday to 8 PM PDT Tuesday.

* IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat
illnesses.
properties.instruction:
Drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, stay out of
the sun, and check up on relatives and neighbors.

Take extra precautions when outside. Wear lightweight and loose
fitting clothing. Try to limit strenuous activities to early morning
or evening. Take action when you see symptoms of heat exhaustion and
heat stroke.

To reduce risk during outdoor work, the Occupational Safety and
Health Administration recommends scheduling frequent rest breaks in
shaded or air conditioned environments. Anyone overcome by heat
should be moved to a cool and shaded location. Heat stroke is an
emergency! Call 9 1 1.

properties.description:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in Indiana...

East Fork White River at Seymour.

East Fork White River near Rivervale.

.Heavy rainfall during the first half of this past week of two to
three inches has produced minor flooding on the East Fork White
River at Seymour and Rivervale.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...East Fork White River near Rivervale.

* WHEN...Until early Tuesday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...At 20.5 feet, Lawrenceport Road just east of
Lawrenceport and on the south side of river begins to flood around
this level. During typical floods in this area this portion of the
road can be flooded by 5 to more than 10 feet of water. During the
January 2005 flood this area would have been near the "bottom" of
the river, underwater by nearly 17 feet. In the Great March 1913
flood, more than 25 feet of water would have covered this location.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:30 PM EDT Sunday the stage was 20.8 feet.
- Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours
ending at 9:30 PM EDT Sunday was 20.9 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
early Tuesday morning and continue falling and remain below
flood stage.
- Flood stage is 20.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Additional information is available at www.weather.gov/ind.

The next statement should be issued Monday afternoon by around 145
PM EDT.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued May 10 at 8:50PM CDT until May 10 at 11:45PM CDT by NWS Austin/San Antonio TX
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of south central Texas, including the following
counties, Burnet and Llano.

* WHEN...Until 1145 PM CDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 844 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms
producing brief torrential rainfall rates of 2 to 3 inches
per hour within the advised area. This will cause urban and
small stream flooding. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain has
already fallen with the highest totals between Llano, Tow,
and Inks Lake State Park.
- Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected
over the area. This additional rain will result in minor
flooding.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Burnet, Marble Falls, Kingsland, Llano, Buchanan Dam,
Gainesville, Granite Shoals, Horseshoe Bay, Buchanan Lake
Village, Highland Haven, Stolz, Bluffton, Lone Grove, Tow,
Inks Lake State Park, Baby Head, Longhorn Cavern State Park,
Valley Spring, Field Creek and Sunrise Beach Village.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

Tuesday morning and continue falling to 7.8 feet Friday
evening.

properties.description:
* AFFECTED AREA...In South Dakota, Stanley, Jones and Lyman.

* WINDS...South 10 to 20 mph with gusts up to 30 mph.

* RELATIVE HUMIDITY...As low as 19 percent.

* IMPACTS...Any fires that ignite will spread rapidly and become
difficult to control or suppress.
properties.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued May 10 at 8:18PM CDT until May 10 at 9:45PM CDT by NWS Fort Worth TX
properties.description:
* WHAT...Urban and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of central Texas, including the following
counties, Coryell and McLennan.

* WHEN...Until 945 PM CDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. This
will likely cause urban and small stream flooding. Low lying
and/or poor drainage areas will experience minor flooding in
the advisory area. Between 1 and 3 inches of rain have
fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Waco, Gatesville, Hewitt, Robinson, Woodway, McGregor,
Lorena, Bruceville-Eddy, Moody, Crawford, Mother Neff State
Park, Fort Gates, Belton Lake, Lake Waco, Oglesby, South
Mountain, Whitson, Osage, Leon Junction and Mound.
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.
